name: PET Bottle
description: Single-use beverage packaging — oil/biomass feedstock to bottle, then landfill, reuse as refill, or bottle-to-bottle recycling.

stages.details: Most PET starts as naphtha/natural-gas liquids refined into PTA and MEG. Bio-based MEG exists but is a small share of global bottle resin.
Resin is injection-molded into preforms, stretch-blow molded into bottles, labeled, filled, and capped. Lightweighting reduces resin per bottle but can hinder reuse.
Bottles move on pallets through cold chains and stores. Single-serve formats maximize packaging per liter delivered.

stages.summary: Litter or landfill after one use
stages.details: Without collection, bottles persist for decades, fragment into microplastics, or clog waterways. Landfill sequesters carbon poorly compared with closed loops.
Deposit-return refillable glass/PET schemes and personal reusable bottles cut virgin resin demand when logistics are local and wash cycles are efficient.
Sorted clear PET is washed, flaked, and re-polymerized or solid-stated into food-grade rPET. Colored/contaminated streams often downcycle to fiber or strapping.
